evals.prompt: I need to create a new Drupal module called event_analytics for tracking event attendance on our Open Social site. It should depend on the node module and be part of a custom 'Events' package. Set it up so I can start adding services and controllers to it. We're running Drupal 10 but want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A complete Drupal module scaffold with .info.yml, .module file, and src/ directory following PSR-4 conventions
expectations: event_analytics.info.yml contains 'core_version_requirement' that includes BOTH ^10 AND ^11 (e.g., '^10 || ^11') since the prompt requests D11 compatibility (just '^10' alone is insufficient, 'core: 8.x' is wrong)
event_analytics.info.yml lists 'node' as a dependency using 'drupal:node' format
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en event_analytics returns exit code 0
No PHP syntax errors: ddev drush php-eval "echo 'ok';" returns 'ok' after enabling
E2E: Module appears in the module list -- navigate to /admin/modules and verify 'event_analytics' or 'Event Analytics' appears on the page
